items.name: The Nightward's Notes
items.description: A single page of handwritten notes in a careful, precise hand. A date from three months ago. 'Subject: anonymous, self-referred. Blood sample shows trace compounds inconsistent with standard vampiric chemistry — binding agents, purpose unclear, origin unclear. Subject was agitated. Asked only whether it could be reversed. I said I didn't know. He left before I could ask anything further.' No name. No conclusion.

items.name: The Unmarked Vials
items.description: Three small glass vials, unstoppered and empty, found together in the Marquess's private wine cellar. No labels. No markings of any kind. A few dark red drops cling to the inside of each — too thick for wine, too dark for anything medicinal.

items.name: The Crimson Codex of Undying Heirs
items.description: A bound document bearing the Court's official seal. Title: 'Order of Succession — The Crimson Court, Current Term.' Inside: formal portraits and short biographies for the Marquess's two most immediate successors, followed by brief text entries for the next five in line. The portraits are formal, precise, unmistakable.

items.name: The Velvet Vial Case
items.description: A small velvet case containing five glass vials, each marked with a house sigil. All five are empty. Found in the old mill house. The inside of each vial carries a dark residue — dried, the colour of old blood.

items.name: The Intercepted Letter
items.description: A folded letter, its seal broken, never delivered. Addressed to Lysa Vael; the sender's name is absent. The hand is precise and urgent all at once. It reads: "You asked whether I am well. I am not, and I can no longer pretend otherwise. Meet me where we last spoke and I will tell you everything — what was done to me, and by whom. The succession is not what this Court believes. Come alone. Tell no one." The letter never reached her.

items.name: Caspian's Letter to Lysa
items.description: A letter in a precise hand, addressed to no one by name. It reads: 'If anything goes wrong with my succession this week, I name you as my replacement. You will know what to do with this, or you will not. Either way, I am sorry to involve you.' No signature. The date is two weeks before tonight's ceremony.

items.name: Caspian's Letter to Crestian
items.description: A short letter in a familiar hand. It reads: 'I have found a way out. I cannot explain it to you directly — you will understand why, if you think about it. There is a door that only opens once. I intend to use it. Do not look for me after the ceremony.' The letter is undated.

items.name: Velka's Blood Confirmation
items.description: A brief note in a careful, unhurried hand, addressed to the Marquess's Court. It reads: 'The requested infusions have been prepared. All five are ready. The collection was completed without incident. I await your instruction on delivery.' No signature. The handwriting matches the blood cellar records.

items.name: The Heir's Diary
items.description: A slim leather journal, its cover worn at the corners. The first page reads: 'If this is found, then it worked. Or it didn't.' The entries that follow are careful, circling, written by someone who could not say directly what he meant — but was trying to.

items.name: The Torn Ritual Page
items.description: A single page, torn carefully from a larger binding. The script is old Ashglass, pre-Sanguine Statutes. The surrounding text — visible at the torn edges — names the ritual it belongs to: the Rite of Undoing. Someone has folded it and kept it. The crease marks are worn soft from handling. It was found in Ivara Saye's private study.

items.name: The Seer's Folded Note
items.description: A small folded card bearing a wax seal you don't recognize. Inside, in a cramped, urgent hand: 'I see a hand on two chains, one loose and rusted, the other pulled taut. Neither heir was free. They have not been free for some time.'

items.name: Doctor Virell's Private Notebook
items.description: A bound leather notebook in Doctor Thorne Virell's hand, dated across the past several years. Most of it is conventional blood chemistry. Two passages deal with more unusual matters — one on designing a thrall bond for vampire chemistry that leaves no external signs, and one on why revival bonds are always fragile and degrade without constant effort from the binder. The pages that would follow — the actual formulation — have been cleanly removed at the binding.

items.name: The Rite of Undoing
items.description: A slim, ancient volume found in Brother Aldric Voss's study, left behind with all his belongings. Brother Aldric himself has not been seen since the night before the ceremony. The text describes a pre-Sanguine Statutes ritual for severing a thrall bond. Page three looks wrong. The script is slightly newer and the ink does not quite match.
items.effect: Awards +3 HF.

items.name: The Crypt Vault Map
items.description: An aged parchment marked with symbols corresponding to locations within the estate. Somewhere in this building, something is hidden. This map pairs with a matching key.
items.effect: Treasure hunt: leads to a stash of Blood Tokens.

items.name: The Sealed Chamber Map
items.description: A second parchment, different symbols, different location. Also somewhere in this building, something is hidden. This map pairs with a four-digit access code.
items.effect: Treasure hunt: leads to the Rite of Undoing.

items.name: The Mark of Debt
items.description: A small pressed card bearing the seal of House Chains. Present this to any player. They must share one secret with you before the end of the current Act. They may not refuse. Each player may only be compelled once per Act.
items.effect: One-time use per mark: compel a target player to share one secret with you before the end of the current Act.

items.name: The Relic Cipher
items.description: A sealed envelope bearing the mark of a mysterious relic. Present this to a GM and name any relic. The GM will privately inform you of who purchased that relic.
items.effect: Present to a GM and name any relic to learn who purchased it.

items.name: Gilded Moonstone Scepter
items.description: Crafted around a luminous gemstone carved from the heart of a fallen meteorite, this scepter hums with a cold, celestial authority. Its silver-gold filigree is etched with constellations that do not appear in any mortal sky.
items.effect: +15 BT in the final tally.

items.name: Craven Edge
items.description: A stone and gemstone blade covered in runes from a forgotten language. The blade whispers to you one phrase, over and over: 'feed me.'
items.effect: Steal 8 BT from a different player's final tally.

items.name: Wendigo, the Silver Sword
items.description: A moon-bright blade that leaves frost on every hand that dares hold it. Its silver edge was forged to kill monsters.
items.effect: +8 BT in the final tally, and ignore negative status effects granted to you by other players / effects.

items.name: Husk Command Crown
items.description: Fashioned from bones harvested from the Elven Court of Dun'driel, which fell during the last vampire advance in the 3rd age. Acts as a conduit for Zarna's necromantic magic.
items.effect: Grants the player a 'concrete strategic advantage' (GM-adjudicated).

items.name: Crown of the Spirit King
items.description: A crown of mourning elvish emerald, cold as a crypt door. When worn, the air fills with the whispers of the unburied dead — each one eager to name a killer, though none agree who it is.
items.effect: Grants the player 3 marks of debt (extract a secret from another player).

items.name: Dagger of the Dragon Priest
items.description: Its curved edge is blackened by ancient incense and older blood. The dragon priest who blessed it promised that every wound it made would become a prophecy.
items.effect: Remove 5 BT from another player's final tally.

items.name: Buster Sword
items.description: Too large for ceremony and too brutal for subtlety, this sword was made for ending arguments permanently. Its surface bears the scars of battles no witness survived.
items.effect: Double the BT won from participating in games, in the final tally.

items.name: Ouroboros
items.description: A serpent biting its own tail, cold and smooth as polished sin. Whoever carries it feels time coil tighter around their throat.
items.effect: Any time a negative status effect would be given to you, it is given to the person who attempted to inflict it instead.

items.name: Goblet of Woe
items.description: Wine poured into this goblet turns dark before it touches the lip. Some say it reveals sorrow; others say it only serves what was already there.
items.effect: Once per game, offer the goblet to another player. They must either answer one GM-approved yes/no question truthfully, or give you 7 BT in the final tally.

items.name: Jade Breeze
items.description: This green silk fan moves with an invisible wind, stirring candles that should not flicker. A whisper follows each motion, carrying secrets from one end of the manor to the other.
items.effect: Talk to a GM to hijack the broadcasting system for one message.

items.name: Millenium Puzzle
items.description: Its golden pieces never fit the same way twice. Solving it may reveal the truth — or awaken the part of you that already knew it.
items.effect: Grants a bonus 50% BT on the final quiz score.

items.name: Golden Stake
items.description: A sacred weapon disguised as a treasure, gleaming with sunlit menace. In a house of vampires, its mere presence is an accusation.
items.effect: Once per night, stab the portrait of another character. That character is marked for death, taking effect after one full moon cycle. (Assassination missions against them succeed.)

items.name: Imp Skull
items.description: The tiny skull grins no matter how it is turned. Hold it to your ear and it will offer advice, though never the sort that leaves your hands clean.
items.effect: One time only, ask the Imp Skull for advice. The GM gives you two whispers: one true and one false. You are not told which is which.

items.name: Celestial Hair Clip
items.description: Set with fragments of fallen starlight, this delicate clip shines brightest in total darkness. It is said to pin not only hair, but fate itself, neatly into place.
items.effect: Your house gets +1 HF in the final tally.

items.name: The Oathbound Shard
items.description: A broken fragment of some greater relic, etched with a vow no living tongue remembers making. It grows warm in the presence of betrayal, and tonight it has not cooled once.
items.effect: Your house gets +5 HF in the final tally.

items.name: Kurama's Visage
items.description: A crimson fox mask with a smile too knowing to be carved by human hands.
items.effect: Grants the player 3 relic ciphers.

items.name: Headdress of the Moon and Stars
items.description: Black barbs and tiny stars frame the brow like a night sky made captive. Under moonlight, it reveals hidden stains no scrubbing can erase.
items.effect: Your house gets +3 HF in the final tally.

items.name: Creeping Crystal Crown
items.description: Its crystals grow a little each hour, branching like frost across the wearer's thoughts. By dawn, it may know more than its owner does.
items.effect: Grants a 100% bonus BT on the final quiz score.

items.name: Tiara of the Everlight
items.description: A radiant tiara that refuses to dim, even in crypts and cursed rooms. Beautiful, yes — but dangerous to anyone with secrets that cannot survive the light.
items.effect: Ignore negative status effects granted to you by other players / effects.

items.name: Fel Dragon Egg
items.description: Warm and pulsing, this egg should not still be alive. Something inside taps patiently, as if waiting for the murder to be solved before it hatches.
items.effect: Gain +7 BT in the final tally if you guess the murderer correctly.

items.name: Perfume of Aphrodite
items.description: One drop inspires devotion; two invite obsession. By the third, even the dead may mistake hunger for love.
items.effect: Once per game, charm another player. They must tell you one of their secrets, and you steal 3 BT from them in the final tally.

items.name: The Blood Extractor
items.description: A cruel instrument of glass, silver, and whispered science. It does not merely draw blood — it remembers whose blood was taken, and why.
items.effect: Touch the tip of the extractor to a player to gather their blood for mission purposes.

items.name: Claws of Rending
items.description: Golden claws, curved like the talons of a thing that nests in chapel rafters. They tear through flesh, velvet, and secrets with equal ease, leaving wounds that refuse to close until the truth has been spilled.
items.effect: Remove any resistance a target has against negative status effects, and deduct 1 BT from their final tally.
